observations.title: Always use parameterized queries — never string-concatenate SQL
facts: SQL injection is the most common database vulnerability
Use ? placeholders or named parameters
Block: template literals or string concat in SQL with variables

observations.title: Wrap multi-step operations in transactions
facts: Partial writes leave data inconsistent
Use BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK
Set appropriate isolation level

observations.title: Add indexes for columns used in WHERE, JOIN, and ORDER BY
facts: Missing indexes cause full table scans
Composite indexes for multi-column queries
Don't over-index — each index slows writes

observations.title: Set NOT NULL constraints unless null has a specific meaning
facts: NULL handling is error-prone (NULL != NULL, NULL + 1 = NULL)
Use DEFAULT values where appropriate
Document the meaning of NULL when used

observations.title: Use migrations for all schema changes — never manual DDL
facts: Migrations are versioned, reviewable, and reversible
Include both up and down migration
Test migrations on a copy of production data

observations.title: Batch large INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations
facts: Bulk operations are orders of magnitude faster than row-by-row
Use INSERT INTO ... VALUES (...), (...), (...)
For very large operations, batch in chunks of 1000-5000

observations.title: Never store plaintext passwords or sensitive tokens
facts: Hash passwords with bcrypt/Argon2
Encrypt sensitive data at rest
Use separate encryption keys per tenant if multi-tenant

observations.title: Implement soft deletes for user-facing data
facts: Use deleted_at timestamp
Add to all SELECT queries: WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
Provide hard-delete endpoint for compliance (GDPR)

observations.title: Set connection pool limits and query timeouts
facts: Prevent connection exhaustion under load
Set statement timeout (30s default)
Monitor slow queries with logging

observations.title: Validate foreign key references before INSERT
facts: Enable foreign key enforcement (PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON)
Check that referenced records exist before inserting
Use ON DELETE CASCADE or ON DELETE SET NULL appropriately
